title: [InstCombine] Handle a bitreverse idiom which ends with a bswap
summary: The generalization transformed a specific bswap+bitreverse pattern into a reusable transformation that folds a bswap into a bitreverse when bswap is the root of the expression tree. The original test case showed a manually unrolled bitreverse followed by bswap, which can be simplified to just bitreverse. The patch extends `matchBSwapOrBitReverse` to recognize bswap as a valid root node in the pattern-matching algorithm, enabling it to capture cases where bswap is the final operation. This allows the optimization to apply not only to the original 32-bit case but also to broader scenarios like 64-bit reversals and multi-use operands, as shown in the new test cases. The key insight is to treat bswap as an entry point for the existing bitreverse recognition logic, leveraging the existing infrastructure to eliminate redundant operations. Developers can apply similar strategies by identifying when a high-level operation (like bswap) can be absorbed into a more general transformation (like bitreverse) by extending the pattern matcher's starting conditions.
